title: alcea glabrata var. microcarpa
intro: Alcea glabrata var. microcarpa, also known as the small-fruited Great Willowherb, is a plant species belonging to the mallow family that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Alcea glabrata var. microcarpa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
